description: Having or showing dignity; a quality of composed, worthy presence. 'Thunoa' names the bearing that commands respect—not through force but through inherent nobility.
sound_symbolism: The word breathes with noble presence. 'Thu' opens with thoughtful dignity, composure beginning. 'No' settles with grounded bearing, stability found. Then 'a' opens to acceptance of this state.
grammatical_notes: Functions as adjective: 'thunoa miona' (dignified person). Describes bearing that reflects 'rolia' (worth). Quality honored through 'nelu' (revere).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Dignified presence—the composure that comes from inner peace, not external validation.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Dignified communication—speaking and listening with respect for all participants.
